title: Yakushev's Law of Coordination: The Fundamental Law of Reality as a Fractal Coordination Network
description: We present the complete mathematical derivation of the Yakushev Law of Coordination — the fundamental principle that governs all stable structures in the Universe. The law is formulated in a single statement and is derived from one axiom (fractal scale invariance) and the definition of the coordination protocol YPSDC. Three structural theorems are proved: the minimal dictionary entropy is exactly two bits; the spatial dimension is necessarily three; and time is an emergent measure of coordination imperfection. The universal error law is derived in its correct logarithmic form ε = κ_c α (ln K_eff)^{2/3} with κ_c = 1/3. The algebraic loop constants are S_odd = 1.2, S_even = 0.8. The scaling quantum q = (3/2)^{1/3} generates the universal mass ladder. The fine-structure constant α^{-1} ≈ 137.036 follows from the topological invariant of the compact fibre F_18. The cosmological constant Ω_Λ = 2/3 emerges from the same topology. The electroweak scale is obtained from the Weyl fermion count L = 96 with a geometric regulator (κ_c π_YUCT)^{-1/2} that eliminates any phenomenological fit. All dimensionless parameters of the Standard Model are reduced to the integers 12, 96, 8 and the universal ratio 3/2. The law makes seven concrete, falsifiable predictions across physics, biology and cosmology, and contains no adjustable continuous parameters.

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of the paper 'Yakushev's Law of Coordination: The Fundamental Law of Reality as a Fractal Coordination Network'. The root main file is the English version, while the other languages are full translations of the same content.

derived_predictions.prediction_1: fermion masses are quantized as m_f = m_e q^{N_f}, N_f ∈ ½ℤ
derived_predictions.prediction_4: temperature dependence of gravity: G_eff(T) = G_0[1 + O(T^{0.20})]
derived_predictions.prediction_5: black hole ringdown time scaling: δτ/τ ∝ M^{-0.67}
derived_predictions.prediction_6: protein mass histogram peaks at integer and half‑integer N_f
derived_predictions.prediction_7: synthetic cell with mass on half‑integer node shows superior fitness

theoretical_ingredients.YPSDC_protocol: offline dictionary sharing + online index transmission
theoretical_ingredients.algebraic_loop_derivation: sums of geometric series with β=2/3
theoretical_ingredients.mass_ladder: ln(m/m_e) = N_f ln q with σ = 0.20 scatter

notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of the paper 'Yakushev's Law of Coordination', version 2.5.MOD. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire article, including all mathematical derivations, figures (mass ladder), tables (fermion masses), and references. The English version is the original reference. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'. Multilingual metadata, fundamental constants (β=2/3, q=(3/2)^{1/3}, α⁻¹≈137.036, Ω_Λ=2/3, π_YUCT≈3.1416407865), and seven falsifiable predictions are explicitly declared in the 'custom' block; the primary metadata language is English. The DOI for this work is 10.5281/zenodo.18444599 (master YUCT treatise), while the geometric tension paper uses 10.5281/zenodo.18444598.
